title: Unit Fixtures, Properties, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Leak Evidence · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for unit fixtures, properties, fuzzing, race detection, and leak evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered deterministic Go evidence suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.
Test URL, discovery, parse, normalize, schedule, state, cursor, search, and policy functions with injected clocks and deterministic inputs.
Fuzz XML tokens, URLs, dates, cursors, state transitions, and normalization with bounded invariants and preserved minimal failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Test Nonclaims
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 4997 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label browser models, unit fixtures, loopback HTTP, temporary PostgreSQL, race, fuzz, load, accessibility, platform, and production evidence separately.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Test Nonclaims
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Test Nonclaims: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 5188 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label browser models, unit fixtures, loopback HTTP, temporary PostgreSQL, race, fuzz, load, accessibility, platform, and production evidence separately.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Test Nonclaims.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label browser models, unit fixtures, loopback HTTP, temporary PostgreSQL, race, fuzz, load, accessibility, platform, and production evidence separately.
